model_findings.description: Gemini and Hermes backends embed the entire prompt (potentially multi-kilobyte) into argv via f"--prompt={prompt}" and f"--oneshot={prompt}"; this risks OS ARG_MAX failures, argument parsing breakage on newlines/equals signs, and is inconsistent with the safer stdin approach used by Codex and the post-"--" positional arg used by Claude.

ChatCompletionRequest accepts arbitrary message lists and content without length, count, or total-size constraints; combined with synchronous subprocess spawning and 120s backend timeout, this creates a straightforward DoS vector via large or numerous requests before any uvicorn/FastAPI defaults intervene.

The --host argument defaults to 127.0.0.1 but is fully configurable; nothing prevents a user from supplying 0.0.0.0, and the resulting server has no authentication, TLS, or request-origin checks, silently exposing all agent CLIs to the network despite documentation claiming localhost-only operation.

FastAPI app and uvicorn invocation impose no worker, semaphore, or queue limits on concurrent /v1/chat/completions calls; each request forks a full CLI subprocess with its own 120s timeout, allowing resource exhaustion under modest parallel load.

The OpenAI-compatible response always returns usage with zero tokens regardless of actual backend output; while documented, this deviates from the spec expectation that real token counts are supplied when available and may break clients that rely on usage fields for budgeting or logging.
